mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects contemporaneous consumer demand strength that translates directly into corporate revenue surprises, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to exhibit a positive Rank IC with 20-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of top-quintile RSAFS months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% indicates sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt-service costs for large-cap corporates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the policy level gets repriced.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 55% of observations over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls captures labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate wage income and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that neither variable alone predicts as reliably.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of months where both conditions are met, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth following two consecutive quarters of negative growth marks an inflection in recession probability that triggers multiple expansion, producing strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings estimates stabilize.
failure_condition: Following two consecutive negative quarterly GDPC1 prints, a subsequent positive GDP growth quarter fails to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and raises the required risk premium for equities, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market discounts a more aggressive Federal Reserve response.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL show a positive correlation with 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC > 0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in industrial production above 1% signals a positive supply shock that boosts near-term earnings expectations and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After INDPRO rises more than 1% month-over-month, 5-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of instances within a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes consumer spending pullbacks and negative earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in industrial production and consumer prices generate stagflation risk that compresses equity multiples because the Federal Reserve must tighten policy into a supply-driven expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with joint increases in INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or the interaction term carries the wrong sign in a multivariate regression.
